description_long: This object is a roadside utility pole embedded in a pile of large, irregular gray rocks at its base. The pole is slender and metallic, with a vertical measuring scale marked along its length. Mounted near the top are two rectangular metal signs: the upper sign is diamond-shaped with a speed limit of 50 and a right-turn arrow, while the lower sign is rectangular and blank. The signs are attached with visible bolts, and the pole shows some weathering and rust stains. The rocks at the base appear to stabilize the pole, suggesting a temporary or construction-related installation. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function and visibility for road users.
description: A metal utility pole with speed limit and directional signs, stabilized by a pile of gray rocks at its base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pole, both signs, and the pile of rocks clearly visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the pole and rocks, with the signs mostly edge-on and less readable.
description_view_2: Rear view with the backs of the signs and the rocks at the base, no visible text or symbols.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the pole and rocks from the other side, with signs edge-on.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: No crossarms are present; only two vertically stacked signs are mounted on the pole.
